description:
A senior colleague has asked you to create a tiny conversion table that they can paste into their investigation report.  
    1. Convert the three supplied UTC timestamps to America/New_York time, using the en_US.UTF-8 locale so the month and weekday names (if you print them) would be in English.  
    2. Record the information in a plain-text log file located at  
       /home/user/analysis/timezone_analysis.log  
       (create the directory first if it does not exist).  
    3. Do not alter the machine’s system-wide settings; you may rely on per-command or per-shell mechanisms (e.g. the `TZ` environment variable or `date -d` with an explicit zone).  
    4. Keep your work to **3–4 terminal commands total**: one to make the directory, one or two to generate the converted timestamps, and one to redirect them into the log file.

    Required file-format (the automated tests are exact-string/line-sensitive):

        • File path:  /home/user/analysis/timezone_analysis.log  
        • Line 1:     Current time zone: America/New_York  
        • Line 2:     (must be completely blank)  
        • Line 3:     2023-03-25 14:30 UTC => 2023-03-25 10:30 EDT  
        • Line 4:     2023-11-05 06:15 UTC => 2023-11-05 01:15 EST  
        • Line 5:     2024-01-01 00:00 UTC => 2023-12-31 19:00 EST  
        • A single terminal newline character (<LF>, “\n”) must terminate every line including the last one.  
        • No extra spaces, no tabs, and no additional blank lines.

    Summary of what must exist when you are done:
        • Directory:  /home/user/analysis/    (mode 755 or inherited default)  
        • File:       /home/user/analysis/timezone_analysis.log  containing exactly the five lines shown above.

    Hints (do NOT paste these commands directly):  
        • `mkdir -p …` creates the directory.  
        • `date -u -d '… UTC'` or `TZ='America/New_York' date -d '…' +'%F %R %Z'` are typical patterns.  
        • Redirect (`>`) or append (`>>`) to build the log file in the required order.

    When the log file is present exactly as specified, the automated grader will consider the task complete.
truth:
/home/user/analysis/timezone_analysis.log content (including final newline):

Current time zone: America/New_York
<LF>
<LF>
2023-03-25 14:30 UTC => 2023-03-25 10:30 EDT
<LF>
2023-11-05 06:15 UTC => 2023-11-05 01:15 EST
<LF>
2024-01-01 00:00 UTC => 2023-12-31 19:00 EST
<LF>

(Where <LF> represents the literal single newline character 0x0A.)
name: task_000000_85b653dc
